description_long: This sculpture features two abstract, organic forms rising from a shared rectangular base, both with twisting, undulating shapes reminiscent of natural growths or flowing fabric. The larger form curves upward in a gentle spiral, culminating in a broad, petal-like extension, while the smaller form mirrors this motion with a tighter, more compact twist. The surface is matte and rough, with visible hand-sculpted textures and subtle variations in thickness, giving it a tactile, earthy quality. The color is a uniform off-white, suggesting it is made from plaster or a similar material. The base is flat and slightly irregular, anchoring the dynamic forms above. The overall impression is one of movement and balance, with the two elements interacting in a harmonious, abstract composition.
description: An abstract, off-white sculpture with two twisting, organic forms rising from a rough rectangular base. The surface is matte and textured, evoking natural growth or flowing fabric.
description_view_0: From this view, both twisting forms are visible, with the larger form on the right and the smaller on the left, both rising from the shared base.
description_view_1: This view shows the back of the larger form, highlighting its broad, curved surface and the subtle undulations of its structure.
description_view_2: Here, the two forms are seen in profile, emphasizing their verticality and the dynamic, spiraling motion of the larger form.
description_view_3: This angle reveals the side of the larger form, showing its layered, petal-like structure and the compact twist of the smaller form behind it.
attribute_location_context: No specific setting or environment is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a plain background.
